response.justification: Bogdan has broad software development experience spanning web, mobile, and some embedded work, but is fundamentally misaligned with this role. The most critical disqualifier is the complete absence of Japanese language proficiency, which is listed as a hard requirement. Beyond that, his technical background is dominated by TypeScript/Node.js web development with no demonstrated experience in embedded firmware, network switch security, L2/L3 networking protocols, or the specific security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal) required for the role. His C experience is minimal and dated, limited to a brief stint at National Instruments and academic projects.
strengths: 15+ years of software development experience demonstrating adaptability across diverse domains and tech stacks
Some C/C++ exposure via National Instruments LabWindows and embedded-adjacent projects (robotic arms, Sound Position Server)
Demonstrated Linux familiarity and experience building/maintaining CI infrastructure and development environments
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency whatsoever — a non-negotiable hard requirement for this position
Zero experience with embedded networking, LAN-switch firmware, or the required security protocols (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal, encryption)
Primary technical stack is TypeScript/Node.js web development with no meaningful Linux kernel work or embedded firmware development
